pattern: DP with a monotonic deque tracks the best reachable previous state within k steps in O(1) per element.
hints: Define dp[i] as the maximum subsequence sum ending at index i; it equals nums[i] plus the best dp[j] for j in [i-k, i-1] that is positive.
You need the maximum of dp[i-k..i-1] efficiently — a sliding-window maximum via a monotonic deque gives O(1) per step.
After building the dp array, the answer is max(dp) since the subsequence must be non-empty.

edge_inputs.description: all negative — must pick the single largest element
k equals array length — can pick any subsequence
alternating large positive and large negative with k=2
large negatives between two distant positives — only reachable if k is large enough
skipping negatives via k window to chain positives

stress.description: 100000 elements with k=50, mix of positive and negative
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    nums = [rng.randint(-10000, 10000) for _ in range(size)]
    k = 50
    return [nums, k]
